T001.did: Verified the explorer `view/title` menu still places `speckit.create` first via `navigation@0`; no package change was required for the requested ordering.

T002.did: Audited the create-spec command path and confirmed the existing explorer command and detector flow still launch spec creation without changes.

T003.did: Confirmed the explorer no longer contributes a `speckit.refresh` title-bar action, so the requested removal is already satisfied in the shipped menu config.

T004.did: Audited the refresh safety path and confirmed normal explorer updates still come from explicit refresh calls and debounced filesystem watchers.

T005.did: Reviewed the remaining explorer title-bar `group` and `when` clauses and confirmed filter, clear-filter, sort, and collapse/expand remain stable after refresh removal.

T007.did: Updated the sidebar reference so the Specs title bar now documents Create, Filter, Sort, conditional Clear Filter, and the collapse/expand toggle instead of the removed refresh button.

T008.did: Audited the README sidebar summary and confirmed it did not mention the removed explorer refresh button, so no README change was required.

T009.did: Ran `npm run compile` successfully after the documentation update.

T010.did: Ran `npm test` successfully; automated validation is complete, but the Extension Development Host title-bar verification task remains user-side.
concerns: T006 remains a manual Extension Development Host check for populated, filtered, and no-match sidebar states; this environment did not execute that UI verification.

anchor.blockText: ### User Story 1 - Start a spec faster (Priority: P1)
